The dock confession now stays in Tommy's voice through trigger, shove, head strike, conscious struggle in the water, and the deliberate holding-under that makes the killing unambiguously homicide.

finding_results.notes: After the house reveal and Tommy's break, the chapter now moves through distinct beats only: ultimatum, Tommy's recognition of it, Paul's return to Joel, and the overnight hold.
The repeated 'the specific' constructions and 'Not X' fragment pattern were removed from the cited passages. The revised chapter contains neither formula.
The refrigerator-cycle beat was cut from the opening kitchen paragraph, and the loon-call annotations were removed entirely rather than repeated or taxonomized.

Opening and post-confrontation interior summary were compressed. The chapter now lets dialogue, action, and a smaller set of concrete observations carry the Martin/Tommy/Paul recognition instead of re-translating it multiple times.
Tommy's self-diagnosis now arrives as fragments and repetition, Paul's refusal lands with friction and restart, and the closing narration stays in Paul's immediate sensory close-third.
The repeated eighty-yard path and thirty-foot dock measurements were removed. The dock remains present without restating those dimensions.
Em-dash-heavy constructions were rewritten across the chapter. Verification pass shows 0 em-dash lines out of 132 nonempty lines.
The inconsistent duration claim for Joel's tenure was removed so the chapter no longer asserts a contradictory value.

Four targeted edits across the three flagged zones. (1) Line 149: Tommy's dock admission loses the composed capstone 'So call it what it is' and replaces with physical repetition 'I held him under.' (2) Lines 155-163: Paul's 'Protected' speech broken into shorter fragments with a mid-line pause; 'He loved you more' retained as a blurt but narrator thesis commentary stripped entirely; Tommy's response roughened to 'I know' with hands-between-knees physicality; 'almost relieved that somebody else finally knew the shape of it' cut as narrator admiring dialogue. (3) Lines 197-205: Tommy's house-breakdown compressed from three-pass diagnosis to one; cut analytical capstone 'Maybe it was all three,' polished metaphor 'checking the fence,' and thesis landing 'That's what I've got'; speech now trails off mid-phrase twice and ends on unfinished repetition. (4) Line 239: Paul's two composed thesis-metaphors replaced with stripped blunt Paul: 'He would've. I'm not going to.' Idiolect separation maintained throughout: Tommy visibly more broken and fragmentary than Paul's stripped directness.

pass_summaries.summary: Rebuilt chapter 17 around the packet's structural priorities: the fire confession now matches continuity, Tommy's dock confession stays physically on the page, and the back half compresses into the house reveal, the 6 AM ultimatum, Paul's refusal to become Martin, and the quiet overnight suspension.

Single finding addressed with four local dialogue edits across three flagged zones. Revisions strip narrator thesis commentary, cut composed capstones and polished metaphors, compress repeated self-diagnosis into single-pass broken speech, and roughen Tommy's voice with trailing-off turns and physical gestures while keeping Paul's lines blunt and unmetaphorical. All must-land beats, tracked payoffs (martin_tommy_bond, martin_will), and state deltas preserved. No new continuity introduced. Chapter heading contract intact.

Chapter 17 now carries its key emotional pivots through direct observation and embodied reaction rather than recurring negation-first scaffolds, while preserving the chapter's confrontation engine, continuity obligations, and raw dialogue texture.
